title: orobanche alba subsp. major
intro: Orobanche alba subsp. major, also known as the parasitic broomrape, is a perennial plant that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. It is a hemiparasitic plant, which means it derives some of its nutrients by parasitizing the roots of other plants, typically legumes. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The herbal preparations of Orobanche alba subsp. major include infusions, tinctures, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
